name: Fine-Tuning Workflow
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI Fine-Tuning
services.purpose: Supervised fine-tuning of GPT-4o-mini and GPT-4o with custom JSONL datasets

services.name: Azure OpenAI Inference
services.purpose: Hosting fine-tuned model deployments for production inference

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Experiment tracking, model registry, evaluation pipeline orchestration

enterprise.note: MLOps pipelines, data labeling, audit logs

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Training data (JSONL), model checkpoints, evaluation results

services.name: Azure AI Foundry
services.purpose: Prompt Flow evaluation pipelines — groundedness, relevance, coherence scoring

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys, storage credentials, model registry secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Fine-tuned model performance tracking — latency, accuracy drift, token usage

optimization_tips: Start with GPT-4o-mini fine-tuning — 10x cheaper than GPT-4o with 80% of quality for many tasks
Use data quality over quantity — 500 high-quality examples often outperform 5,000 noisy ones
Run evaluation before and after fine-tuning to quantify improvement — avoid unnecessary retraining
Use LoRA/QLoRA for open-source models on AML compute — avoids full-weight fine-tuning costs
Schedule training runs during off-peak hours for lower spot pricing on AML compute
Delete unused fine-tuned model deployments — each hosted model incurs hourly costs even when idle
